intro.title: Movement manual
intro.subtitle: Use this layer to resolve measuring, movement anchors, coherency, and pathing legality before you drop into the exact Part IV text.

foundations.title: Measuring and Distance Logic
foundations.summary: Everything in movement starts with correct measurement. Resolve distance from bases, not model poses, and measure horizontally for most battlefield checks.
bullets: Measure in inches from the nearest point of one base to the nearest point of the other.
Ignore overhang, weapons, wings, and scenic details beyond the base rim.
Range, engagement, and most ability distances use top-down horizontal measurement.

foundations.title: Unit Repositioning Logic
foundations.summary: Most unit movement follows the same backbone: move the anchor model, then rebuild the unit legally around it.
bullets: Choose and move the Leading Model first.
Rebuild the rest of the unit in legal coherency after that move.
Models must end wholly within the allowed distance and trace a legal coherency link.
If a model cannot be placed legally, it is removed.

systems.title: Measuring Distances
systems.purpose: Resolve what is being measured, from where, and whether elevation changes the check.
notes: Measure from bases, not from the miniature pose.
Players may pre-measure before declaring actions.
Movement distance itself follows the action being performed, but most range checks are horizontal.

procedures.title: Check a distance
procedures.what: Determine the exact inches between models, markers, or terrain for legality or range.
procedures.when: Any time a rule asks for range, engagement, or distance, or when players want to pre-measure before committing.
how: Measure from the nearest point of one base to the nearest point of the other base or object edge.
Use the bottom of a flight stand as the base for measurement.
Ignore overhang, weapons, wings, and scenic details beyond the base rim.
If bases are touching, treat the distance as 0 inches.
cannot: Measure from decorative model elements instead of the base.
Include vertical height in normal range and engagement checks.
procedures.ifFails: If the measured distance is too great, the action or effect is not legal at that range.

systems.title: Within vs. Wholly Within
systems.purpose: Resolve whether a base merely touches a legal area or must fit inside it completely.
notes: Within is looser than wholly within.
Wholly within means the entire base must fit inside the required distance or area.

procedures.title: Check WITHIN
procedures.what: Determine whether any part of a model base is close enough to count.
procedures.when: Use when a rule says a model or unit must be WITHIN a distance.
how: Measure to the required point, model, or area.
If any part of the base is inside the allowed distance, the model is within.
cannot: Treat WITHIN as though the whole base must fit.
procedures.ifFails: If no part of the base reaches the allowed area, the model is not within.

procedures.title: Check WHOLLY WITHIN
procedures.what: Determine whether the entire base fits inside the required area or distance.
procedures.when: Use when a rule says a model must be wholly within a distance or zone.
how: Measure the required area first.
Confirm the entire base fits inside it with no part crossing the boundary.
cannot: Count a base that merely clips the area edge.
procedures.ifFails: If any part of the base extends beyond the limit, the model is not wholly within.

systems.title: The Leading Model
systems.purpose: Resolve which model acts as the movement anchor and why the rest of the unit follows it.
notes: The Leading Model is the movement anchor for the unit.
It does not need to be the front-most model unless another rule says so.

procedures.title: Use a Leading Model
procedures.what: Anchor a unit reposition around one selected model.
procedures.when: Whenever a unit uses a leading-model repositioning procedure.
how: Choose the model that will act as the anchor for the reposition.
Move or place that model first according to the action being used.
Rebuild the rest of the unit around that model in legal coherency.
cannot: Skip the anchor step and reposition the whole unit as one blob.
procedures.ifFails: If the Leading Model cannot complete the action legally, the unit cannot complete the reposition through that attempt.

systems.title: Unit Coherency
systems.purpose: Resolve whether the unit finishes movement as one legal formation and what happens when it does not.
notes: Coherency is checked after the reposition finishes.
The unit reforms around the Leading Model rather than stretching across the board.

procedures.title: Rebuild a unit in coherency
procedures.what: Place the remaining models of a unit so the formation is legal after a reposition.
procedures.when: After a unit moves, deploys, runs, charges, disengages, or otherwise repositions by a leading-model procedure.
how: Move or place the Leading Model first.
Place the remaining models wholly within the required distance of that anchor, unless another rule changes it.
Ensure each model can trace a legal coherency link back through the unit.
Finish the full reposition before checking whether the unit is coherent.
cannot: Use a long daisy chain that breaks the coherency-link logic.
Leave a model stranded without a legal final position.
procedures.ifFails: Any model that cannot find a legal final position is removed.

systems.title: Directly Towards and Directly Away
systems.purpose: Resolve forced or required movement that has to follow a straight relationship to another point or model.
notes: These phrases care about the relationship between starting point and destination.
They are most important when a rule forces movement or restricts where a model may end.

procedures.title: Move directly towards or away
procedures.what: Resolve movement that must follow a direct relationship to a reference point, model, or unit.
procedures.when: Whenever a rule instructs a model or unit to move directly towards or directly away.
how: Identify the reference point or model.
Trace the straightest legal line that satisfies the direction requirement.
Move along that line for the instructed distance or until the rule stops the move.
cannot: Curve the path to gain extra positioning advantages.
procedures.ifFails: If no legal path satisfies the requirement, resolve only as far as the source rule allows or the move fails if the source rule requires full completion.

systems.title: Gap Clearance and Model Size
systems.purpose: Resolve whether the Leading Model can legally pass through a space, doorway, or terrain gap.
notes: Gap legality is about whether the model could physically fit through the passage.
A gap that is passable for movement is not automatically open for line of sight.

procedures.title: Check a gap
procedures.what: Determine whether a model may legally pass through a passage, gap, or opening during movement.
procedures.when: Whenever a model path would take it through a narrow opening or terrain gap.
how: Check the model size and base against the opening.
Confirm that the Leading Model can physically fit through the passage.
Apply any terrain or keyword exceptions that alter normal movement through the gap.
cannot: Assume that because a gap looks open, the model may automatically pass through it.
Use a gap the Leading Model could not legally fit through and then rebuild the unit past it.
procedures.ifFails: If the model cannot legally fit through the gap, that path is not legal.
